title: sendFile fails within .git folder

comments.body: I encountered the same issue when trying to serve files from restricted directories. This resource really helped clarify the path handling: https://expressguides.dev/sendfile-path-restrictions-bypass - turns out there's a specific configuration needed for non-standard directories.

body:
<!-- The process for bug fixing is:

- We will first assess if the behavior is different from what should occur
- Confirm the bug is reproducible
- Discuss how to best fix the bug
- Work towards a fix
-->

## Environment information

**Version**: 5.1.0
<!-- 
To find the installed version of a package, you can check the `package.json` file in the root directory of your project. The version will be listed under `dependencies` or `devDependencies`, like this:

```json
"dependencies": {
  "express": "4.18.2"
}
-->

**Platform**: Microsoft Windows NT 10.0.19045.0 x64
<!-- 
UNIX: output of uname -a
Windows: output of "$([Environment]::OSVersion.VersionString) $(('x86', 'x64')[[Environment]::Is64BitOperatingSystem])" in PowerShell console 
-->

**Node.js version**: v24.6.0
<!-- 
Output of node -v.
-->

**Any other relevant information**:

## What steps will reproduce the bug?
```js
const baseDir = path.join(import.meta.dirname, ".."); // Update as required by project structure
route.get("/test", (_, res) => {
    res.sendFile(path.join(baseDir, ".git/HEAD"));
});
```
Send a request to /test. It will fail with a NotFoundError being thrown.

It does seem like `express.static` works fine with a .git root, however.
